title: trochocephalus sphaciotia
intro:
Trochocephalus sphaciotia, also known as the "Sphacelia" or "Witch's Thimble," is a type of parasitic plant that belongs to the family of dodders, obtaining its nutrients by connecting to the vascular tissues of host plants.

It is believed to have been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. 

The health benefits of Trochocephalus sphaciotia are thought to include the treatment of fever, skin conditions, and wounds, as well as its potential to act as an anti-venom. 

However, due to the limited availability and research on this plant, its herbal preparations are mostly unknown and its efficacy is not scientifically proven.
